name: TitleMax Title Pawns
description_short: TitleMax offers vehicle title pawns and loans secured by your car or motorcycle title — same-day cash up to $25,000, no credit check required.
description_long:
TitleMax was founded in 1998 in Columbus, Georgia and has grown into one of the largest title lending companies in the United States. Operating under parent company TMX Finance LLC, headquartered in Savannah, Georgia, TitleMax runs 1,000+ stores across 18 states. The company is a for-profit commercial lender with no CDFI, HUD-approved, or NFCC nonprofit certifications. TitleMax filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2009 and exited in 2010. In 2023, an acquisition by CCF Holdings was announced. Its brands also include TitleBucks, InstaLoan, and EquityAuto Loan.

TitleMax's core products are auto title loans, auto title pawns (a legally distinct structure used in Georgia and select states where the lender technically holds the title during repayment), motorcycle title loans and pawns, and personal installment loans available in select states including Arizona, Delaware, Kansas, Mississippi, Missouri, Nevada, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, and Wisconsin. Loan amounts vary by state — up to $25,000 for qualified borrowers, with Mississippi capped at $3,250 and Tennessee at $6,500. Approval is based primarily on vehicle value rather than credit score, making it accessible to borrowers with poor or no credit history. Funds can be disbursed in as little as 30 minutes. Customers retain use of their vehicle throughout the loan term.

TitleMax's primary differentiators are scale and speed. With 1,000+ locations, it's one of the most accessible title lenders in the country. The same-day, in-store approval model requires no credit history, and the company accepts motorcycles in addition to cars and trucks. A mobile app for Android and iOS allows customers to make payments, check balances and due dates, and find store locations. Online applications can be started digitally, though final approval typically requires an in-store visit.

The principal limitation is cost: APRs typically range from 100% to 300%+, making these among the most expensive forms of consumer credit available. TitleMax has a significant regulatory track record — a 2023 CFPB enforcement action resulted in a $10 million civil penalty and $5.05 million in consumer redress for violating the Military Lending Act by charging active-duty military members nearly three times the legal 36% APR cap. Additional enforcement actions were taken by the Pennsylvania Attorney General in 2022 ($1.6M restitution), California DBO in 2019 ($25,000 settlement), and Illinois regulators between 2013 and 2015 ($527,000+ in fines). TitleMax is not BBB-accredited and carries numerous consumer complaints. This product functions best as an emergency last resort for unbanked or credit-poor borrowers — not as a routine financial tool.
address: 3247 S Cobb Dr SE, Smyrna, GA 30080

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee applicable to this financial product.

pros: Same-day funding in as little as 30 minutes — one of the fastest cash disbursement models available
No credit check required — approval based on vehicle value, not credit score or history
Customers keep and drive their vehicle throughout the full loan repayment period
Over 1,000 store locations across 18 states — highly accessible footprint for in-person service
Offers motorcycle title loans and pawns in addition to standard auto products
No prepayment penalty — borrowers can pay off the loan early without additional fees
Mobile app (Android and iOS) enables payments, balance checks, and account management without store visits
cons: Extremely high APRs — typically 100% to 300%+ annually, far above banks, credit unions, or even most payday lenders
2023 CFPB enforcement action: $10M civil penalty plus $5.05M consumer redress for illegally overcharging active-duty military families
Vehicle title is at risk — failure to repay can result in vehicle repossession, removing a borrower's primary transportation
Not BBB-accredited and subject to numerous consumer complaints; additional state enforcement actions in PA, CA, and IL
First payment is 30 days of interest charged upfront, increasing the immediate cost of borrowing
best_for: Vehicle owners with poor or no credit history who cannot qualify for traditional bank or credit union loans
Borrowers facing emergency expenses who need cash the same day and own a paid-off or nearly paid-off vehicle
Subprime consumers in one of TitleMax's 18 operating states who need to retain use of their vehicle while accessing its equity
Borrowers who have been turned down by conventional personal loan lenders and need a collateral-based alternative
diagnosis: TitleMax is genuinely useful only for vehicle owners with poor or no credit who face urgent cash needs and have no lower-cost options available. The main caveat is severe: triple-digit APRs, a documented pattern of regulatory violations including a $10M federal penalty for overcharging military families, and the real risk of losing your vehicle to repossession make this a high-stakes financial product that should be treated as a last resort rather than a routine borrowing tool.
services: Auto Title Pawns (Georgia and select states)
Personal Installment Loans (AZ, DE, KS, MS, MO, NV, SC, TN, TX, UT, WI only)
Same-day cash funding (as little as 30 minutes)
Mobile app account management (Android and iOS)
Bad credit and no-credit lending based on vehicle valuation
